subsection.heading: In General.—
subsection.chapeau: The head of each executive agency shall ensure that—

paragraph.chapeau: an interactive telework training program is provided to—

subparagraph.content: employees eligible to participate in the telework program of the agency; and

paragraph.content: except as provided under subsection (b), an employee has successfully completed the interactive telework training program before that employee enters into a written agreement to telework described under section 6502(b)(2);

paragraph.chapeau: teleworkers and nonteleworkers are treated the same for purposes of—

subparagraph.content: periodic appraisals of job performance of employees;
training, rewarding, reassigning, promoting, reducing in grade, retaining, and removing employees;
other acts involving managerial discretion; and

paragraph.content: when determining what constitutes diminished employee performance, the agency shall consult the performance management guidelines of the Office of Personnel Management.

subsection.heading: Training Requirement Exemptions.—
subsection.content: The head of an executive agency may provide for an exemption from the training requirements under subsection (a), if the head of that agency determines that the training would be unnecessary because the employee is already teleworking under a work arrangement in effect before the date of enactment of this chapter.

note.heading: References in Text
note.p: The date of enactment of this chapter, referred to in subsec. (b), is the date of enactment of Pub. L. 111–292, which was approved Dec. 9, 2010.
